API DOCUMENTATION - User Management Service
Version: 2.4.1
Base URL: https://api.techcorp.com/v2

AUTHENTICATION

All requests require authentication via Bearer token:
Authorization: Bearer <your_jwt_token>

Tokens are obtained via POST /auth/login and expire after 15 minutes.
Use POST /auth/refresh to obtain a new token using your refresh token.

---

ENDPOINTS

GET /users
---------
Retrieve a paginated list of users.

Query Parameters:
- page (integer, default: 1) - Page number
- limit (integer, default: 20, max: 100) - Items per page
- sort (string, default: "created_at", options: "created_at", "name", "email") - Sort field
- order (string, default: "desc", options: "asc", "desc") - Sort order
- role (string, optional) - Filter by role: "admin", "editor", "viewer"
- search (string, optional) - Search by name or email

Response: 200 OK
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 42,
      "email": "user@example.com",
      "name": "John Doe",
      "role": "editor",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15T10:30:00Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-04-28T14:22:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 156,
    "total_pages": 8
  }
}

---

POST /users
-----------
Create a new user. Requires admin role.

Request Body:
{
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "name": "Jane Smith",
  "role": "viewer",
  "password": "securepassword123"
}

Validation Rules:
- email: valid email format, must be unique
- name: 2-100 characters
- role: must be one of "admin", "editor", "viewer"
- password: minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase, one lowercase, one number

Response: 201 Created
{
  "id": 43,
  "email": "newuser@example.com",
  "name": "Jane Smith",
  "role": "viewer",
  "created_at": "2026-04-30T09:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-30T09:00:00Z"
}

Error Responses:
- 400 Bad Request - Validation errors
- 401 Unauthorized - Not authenticated
- 403 Forbidden - Insufficient permissions (not admin)
- 409 Conflict - Email already exists

---

GET /users/:id
--------------
Retrieve a single user by ID.

Path Parameters:
- id (integer, required) - User ID

Response: 200 OK
{
  "id": 42,
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "role": "editor",
  "created_at": "2026-03-15T10:30:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-28T14:22:00Z"
}

Error Responses:
- 404 Not Found - User does not exist

---

PATCH /users/:id
----------------
Update user details. Users can update their own profile. Admins can update any user.

Path Parameters:
- id (integer, required) - User ID

Request Body (all fields optional):
{
  "name": "Updated Name",
  "email": "newemail@example.com"
}

Note: Password changes must be done via POST /auth/change-password.
Role changes must be done via PATCH /users/:id/role (admin only).

Response: 200 OK
{
  "id": 42,
  "email": "newemail@example.com",
  "name": "Updated Name",
  "role": "editor",
  "created_at": "2026-03-15T10:30:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-30T11:00:00Z"
}

---

DELETE /users/:id
-----------------
Delete a user. Requires admin role. This is a hard delete - all associated data
(sessions, documents, chat sessions) will also be deleted via CASCADE.

Path Parameters:
- id (integer, required) - User ID

Response: 204 No Content

Error Responses:
- 401 Unauthorized - Not authenticated
- 403 Forbidden - Insufficient permissions (not admin)
- 404 Not Found - User does not exist
- 409 Conflict - Cannot delete user with active admin role (at least one admin must exist)

---

PATCH /users/:id/role
---------------------
Change a user's role. Requires admin role.

Path Parameters:
- id (integer, required) - User ID

Request Body:
{
  "role": "admin"
}

Response: 200 OK
{
  "id": 42,
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "name": "John Doe",
  "role": "admin",
  "created_at": "2026-03-15T10:30:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-04-30T11:00:00Z"
}

Error Responses:
- 400 Bad Request - Invalid role value
- 401 Unauthorized - Not authenticated
- 403 Forbidden - Insufficient permissions (not admin)
- 404 Not Found - User does not exist

---

RATE LIMITING

All endpoints are rate-limited:
- Standard endpoints: 100 requests per minute per IP
- Authentication endpoints: 5 requests per minute per IP
- Admin endpoints: 50 requests per minute per IP

Rate limit headers are included in all responses:
X-RateLimit-Limit: 100
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 95
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1682851200

When rate limit is exceeded:
Response: 429 Too Many Requests
{
  "error": "rate_limit_exceeded",
  "message": "Too many requests. Please try again in 45 seconds.",
  "retry_after": 45
}

---

ERROR RESPONSE FORMAT

All errors follow a consistent format:
{
  "error": "error_code",
  "message": "Human-readable error description",
  "details": { } // Optional additional context
}

Common Error Codes:
- invalid_request - Malformed request body or parameters
- unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication token
- forbidden - Insufficient permissions
- not_found - Resource does not exist
- conflict - Resource already exists or operation conflicts with current state
- validation_error - Request body failed validation (see details for field-level errors)
- rate_limit_exceeded - Too many requests
- internal_error - Unexpected server error
